text:
There was an old woman who lived in a swamp. She never sung because she was too busy crooning little birdies. It wasn't that she didn't like birds—it was just hard to find one big enough for an air melody.

One day, a beautiful star-billed owl flew into her hut. It liked her voice. She became famous.

She sang every night for a while. People loved her.
